description: BS 7671 Regulation Reference Database for EICR/EIC Inspections

regulations.section: Section 1 - Scope, Object and Fundamental Principles
regulations.title: Additions and alterations
regulations.description: Every addition or alteration made to an existing installation shall comply with the regulations and shall not impair the safety of the existing installation.
common_observations: Additions or alterations that do not comply with BS 7671
Alterations that have impaired the safety of the existing installation
Evidence of DIY or unqualified work
regulations.recommended_action: Review and rectify non-compliant additions or alterations to ensure compliance with BS 7671.

regulations.title: Purpose, supplies and structure
regulations.description: Assessment shall be made of the purpose of the installation, supply characteristics and structure of the installation.
common_observations: Installation purpose not determined
Supply characteristics not assessed
Installation structure not documented
regulations.recommended_action: Carry out full assessment of the installation purpose, supply characteristics and installation structure.

regulations.title: External influences
regulations.description: External influences shall be assessed to determine requirements for the selection and erection of equipment.
common_observations: External influences not assessed
Equipment not suitable for the environment
IP rating of equipment not appropriate for location
regulations.recommended_action: Assess external influences and ensure all equipment is suitable for its environment and has appropriate IP rating.

regulations.title: Compatibility
regulations.description: Assessment shall be made of any characteristics of equipment likely to have harmful effects on other equipment or the supply.
common_observations: Harmonic producing equipment causing interference
Equipment not compatible with supply characteristics
Surge protection not provided where required
regulations.recommended_action: Assess compatibility of installed equipment and provide appropriate mitigation measures.

regulations.title: Maintainability
regulations.description: The frequency and quality of maintenance likely to be applied during the intended life of the installation shall be assessed.
common_observations: Installation not maintainable
Access to equipment restricted or obstructed
Maintenance requirements not documented
regulations.recommended_action: Ensure all equipment is accessible for maintenance and inspection purposes.

regulations.title: ADS - Protective earthing
regulations.description: Exposed-conductive-parts shall be connected to a protective conductor under the rules of Section 543.
common_observations: Missing earth connection to exposed-conductive-parts
Earth continuity not confirmed
Protective conductor disconnected or missing
Earthing conductor undersized
regulations.recommended_action: Ensure all exposed-conductive-parts are connected to a protective conductor of adequate size and continuity is confirmed.

regulations.title: ADS - TN systems disconnection times
regulations.description: In a TN system the maximum disconnection time for circuits not exceeding 32A shall be 0.4 seconds at 230V.
common_observations: Disconnection time exceeds 0.4 seconds for final circuits up to 32A
Zs value exceeds maximum permitted for protective device
Earth fault loop impedance too high
regulations.recommended_action: Reduce earth fault loop impedance or install RCD protection to achieve disconnection within 0.4 seconds.

regulations.title: ADS - TN systems distribution circuit disconnection
regulations.description: Maximum disconnection time for distribution circuits in TN systems shall be 5 seconds.
common_observations: Distribution circuit disconnection time exceeds 5 seconds
Zs at distribution board exceeds maximum value
regulations.recommended_action: Reduce earth fault loop impedance to ensure distribution circuit disconnection within 5 seconds.

regulations.title: ADS - TT systems
regulations.description: In TT systems, RCD protection is generally required as the sole means of fault protection. Maximum Zs depends on RCD rating.
common_observations: TT system without RCD protection
RCD not suitable for the earth fault loop impedance
Earth electrode resistance too high
No periodic testing of earth electrode
regulations.recommended_action: Install appropriate RCD protection and verify earth electrode resistance is within acceptable limits for the RCD rating.

regulations.title: Additional protection - RCDs
regulations.description: Additional protection by RCDs with a rated residual operating current not exceeding 30 mA shall be provided for socket-outlets with rated current not exceeding 32A and mobile equipment with rated current not exceeding 32A for outdoor use.
common_observations: Socket-outlets not protected by 30mA RCD
No RCD protection for socket-outlets rated up to 32A
Outdoor socket-outlets without additional protection
Mobile equipment circuits without RCD protection
regulations.recommended_action: Install 30mA RCD protection for all socket-outlet circuits rated up to 32A.

regulations.title: Reduced low voltage (SELV and PELV)
regulations.description: Protection by SELV or PELV requires the nominal voltage not to exceed 50V AC or 120V DC.
common_observations: SELV/PELV system voltage exceeds limits
Separation between SELV and other circuits not maintained
SELV source not suitable
regulations.recommended_action: Verify SELV/PELV systems comply with voltage limits and separation requirements.

regulations.title: Basic protection - Insulation of live parts
regulations.description: Live parts shall be completely covered with insulation which can only be removed by destruction.
common_observations: Damaged or deteriorated insulation on conductors
Live parts accessible due to damaged insulation
Cable sheathing damaged exposing cores
Insulation breakdown at terminations
regulations.recommended_action: Repair or replace damaged insulation to ensure all live parts are fully insulated.

regulations.title: Basic protection - Barriers or enclosures
regulations.description: Live parts shall be inside enclosures or behind barriers providing at least IP2X or IPXXB protection.
common_observations: Live parts accessible without use of a tool
Missing blanking plates in consumer unit
Enclosure damaged allowing access to live parts
Barrier or enclosure not providing adequate IP rating
regulations.recommended_action: Ensure all live parts are behind barriers or within enclosures providing at least IP2X protection.

regulations.title: Nominal voltage - ELV lighting
regulations.description: For extra-low voltage lighting installations, transformers shall comply with relevant standards.
common_observations: ELV lighting transformer not compliant
Incorrect class of transformer used
ELV lighting not properly isolated
regulations.recommended_action: Replace non-compliant transformers with those meeting the appropriate standard.

regulations.title: Protection against fire - Consumer unit enclosures
regulations.description: In domestic premises, consumer units and similar switchgear assemblies shall comply with BS EN 61439-3 and shall be enclosed in a non-combustible enclosure or cabinet.
common_observations: Consumer unit enclosure is not non-combustible material
Plastic consumer unit not enclosed in non-combustible cabinet
Consumer unit does not comply with BS EN 61439-3
Non-metallic consumer unit in domestic premises
regulations.recommended_action: Replace consumer unit with one having a non-combustible enclosure or enclose existing unit in a non-combustible cabinet complying with BS EN 61439-3.

regulations.title: Protection against fire - Arc fault detection
regulations.description: Where the risk of fire is increased due to the nature of processed or stored materials, arc fault detection devices (AFDDs) shall be provided.
common_observations: AFDD not installed where required by risk assessment
AFDD fitted but not functional
regulations.recommended_action: Install AFDD protection where the fire risk assessment indicates it is required.

regulations.title: Protection against fire - Sealing of wiring system penetrations
regulations.description: Where wiring systems pass through elements of building construction having specified fire resistance, the openings remaining shall be sealed to the degree of fire resistance of the element.
common_observations: Fire barriers not sealed where cables penetrate fire-rated walls
Holes in fire-rated construction not properly sealed
Missing fire stopping around cable penetrations
regulations.recommended_action: Seal all cable penetrations through fire-rated elements to restore fire resistance rating.

regulations.title: Protection against fire caused by electrical equipment
regulations.description: Fixed equipment producing arcs, sparks or hot particles shall be enclosed in arc-resistant material or screened from building elements.
common_observations: Equipment likely to produce sparks not suitably enclosed
Combustible materials stored near switchgear
Inadequate clearance from combustible materials
regulations.recommended_action: Ensure adequate clearance from combustible materials and that equipment is suitably enclosed.

regulations.title: Overload protection
regulations.description: The operating characteristics of a device for protection against overload shall satisfy the conditions of Regulation 433.1.1.
common_observations: Overload protective device rating exceeds cable current-carrying capacity
Cable undersized for protective device rating
Overload protection not provided
regulations.recommended_action: Ensure protective device rating does not exceed the current-carrying capacity of the cable it protects.

regulations.title: Fault current protection
regulations.description: A device for protection against fault current shall have a breaking capacity not less than the prospective fault current at the point of installation.
common_observations: Protective device breaking capacity inadequate for prospective fault current
Prospective fault current not determined
Breaking capacity of devices not verified
regulations.recommended_action: Verify prospective fault current and ensure all protective devices have adequate breaking capacity.

regulations.title: Surge protection devices
regulations.description: Where required by the risk assessment, surge protective devices (SPDs) shall be provided.
common_observations: SPD not installed where required by risk assessment
SPD not suitable for the installation
No surge protection assessment carried out
regulations.recommended_action: Carry out surge protection risk assessment and install SPDs where required.

regulations.section: Section 5 - Selection and Erection of Equipment
regulations.title: Compliance with standards
regulations.description: Every item of equipment shall comply with the relevant British Standard or equivalent.
common_observations: Equipment not compliant with relevant standards
Non-standard equipment installed
Equipment markings missing or illegible
regulations.recommended_action: Replace non-compliant equipment with items meeting the relevant British or European Standard.

regulations.section: Section 5 - Selection and Erection of Equipment
regulations.title: Warning notices - Earthing and bonding
regulations.description: A permanent label with the words 'Safety Electrical Connection - Do Not Remove' shall be permanently fixed in a visible position at or near the main earthing terminal and bonding connections.
common_observations: Warning label missing at main earthing terminal
Safety electrical connection label missing from bonding connections
Labels deteriorated or illegible
Main bonding connections not labelled
regulations.recommended_action: Affix permanent warning labels stating 'Safety Electrical Connection - Do Not Remove' at main earthing terminal and all bonding connections.

regulations.section: Section 5 - Selection and Erection of Equipment
regulations.title: Circuit identification and labelling
regulations.description: Every fuse, circuit-breaker and switch shall be arranged and identified so that the circuit protected or controlled can be easily recognised.
common_observations: Circuit chart missing or not up to date
Circuits not identified at consumer unit
Circuit labelling illegible or inaccurate
No schedule of circuits displayed
regulations.recommended_action: Provide an accurate and up-to-date circuit chart/schedule at the consumer unit identifying all circuits.

regulations.section: Section 5 - Selection and Erection of Equipment
regulations.title: Warning notice - Dual supplies
regulations.description: Where an installation includes alternative or additional sources of supply, a warning notice shall be provided.
common_observations: No warning notice for dual supply
Solar PV installation without appropriate warning labels
Battery storage system not labelled
regulations.recommended_action: Provide warning notices indicating the presence of alternative or additional supply sources.

regulations.section: Section 5 - Selection and Erection of Equipment
regulations.title: Warning notice - Non-standard colours
regulations.description: Where an installation includes wiring using non-harmonised cable colours, a warning notice shall be provided.
common_observations: Non-harmonised cable colours present without warning notice
Mixed old and new cable colours without notice
Warning notice for non-standard colours missing at consumer unit
regulations.recommended_action: Provide a warning notice at the consumer unit indicating the presence of non-harmonised (old) cable colours.

regulations.section: Section 5 - Selection and Erection of Equipment
regulations.title: Warning notice - Periodic inspection label
regulations.description: A notice of the date of the next recommended periodic inspection shall be provided at or near the consumer unit.
common_observations: No periodic inspection notice displayed
Periodic inspection date label missing or out of date
Next inspection date not documented
regulations.recommended_action: Provide a notice at or near the consumer unit showing the date of the next recommended periodic inspection.

regulations.section: Section 5 - Selection and Erection of Equipment
regulations.title: Warning notice - RCD quarterly test
regulations.description: Where an RCD is provided, a notice shall be fixed in a prominent position stating that the RCD should be tested quarterly.
common_observations: RCD quarterly test notice missing
No instruction label for RCD testing
RCD test button notice not displayed
regulations.recommended_action: Provide a notice in a prominent position advising that the RCD should be tested quarterly using the test button.

regulations.section: Section 5 - Selection and Erection of Equipment
regulations.title: Electromechanical stresses
regulations.description: Conductors and their connections shall be capable of withstanding without damage the electromechanical forces of any current including fault current.
common_observations: Conductor connections showing signs of overheating
Loose terminations at switchgear
Evidence of arcing at connections
regulations.recommended_action: Check and re-tighten all terminations. Replace any damaged conductors or connections.

regulations.section: Section 5 - Selection and Erection of Equipment
regulations.title: Cables concealed in walls - Protection zones
regulations.description: Where cables are concealed in walls at a depth of less than 50mm, they shall be installed within prescribed zones or protected against mechanical damage.
common_observations: Cables not in prescribed zones and without RCD protection
Concealed cables not protected by 30mA RCD
Cables not run in safe zones
regulations.recommended_action: Provide RCD protection for circuits with cables concealed in walls not in prescribed zones or at a depth of less than 50mm.

regulations.section: Section 5 - Selection and Erection of Equipment
regulations.title: Cables through structural elements
regulations.description: Where cables pass through or are installed in close proximity to thermal insulation, the cable current-carrying capacity shall be derated.
common_observations: Cables surrounded by thermal insulation without derating
Cable current-carrying capacity not derated for insulation
Cables in contact with thermal insulation
Downlights insulation covering not provided
regulations.recommended_action: Derate cable current-carrying capacity or provide suitable separation from thermal insulation. Apply appropriate correction factors.

regulations.section: Section 5 - Selection and Erection of Equipment
regulations.title: Protection against heat
regulations.description: Cables shall be suitable for the highest and lowest temperatures they are likely to encounter during use.
common_observations: Cable rating not suitable for ambient temperature
Cable exposed to excessive heat sources
Thermoplastic cable in contact with hot surfaces
regulations.recommended_action: Ensure cables are rated for the ambient temperature conditions and adequately separated from heat sources.

regulations.section: Section 5 - Selection and Erection of Equipment
regulations.title: Protection against mechanical damage
regulations.description: Cables shall be adequately protected against mechanical damage where they may be exposed to such damage.
common_observations: Cables vulnerable to mechanical damage
No mechanical protection where cables are exposed
Surface-mounted cables in areas accessible to damage
Cable damaged or deteriorated
regulations.recommended_action: Provide adequate mechanical protection for cables or relocate cables to avoid areas where damage is likely.

regulations.section: Section 5 - Selection and Erection of Equipment
regulations.title: Current-carrying capacity
regulations.description: The current-carrying capacity of a conductor shall not be less than the maximum sustained current which it will carry in normal service.
common_observations: Cable undersized for the load it carries
Current-carrying capacity exceeded
Cable derating factors not applied
regulations.recommended_action: Replace undersized cables or reduce the load to within the cable current-carrying capacity.

regulations.section: Section 5 - Selection and Erection of Equipment
regulations.title: Cross-sectional area of conductors
regulations.description: The cross-sectional area of every conductor shall be adequate for normal load current, voltage drop limits and thermal constraints.
common_observations: Voltage drop exceeds permitted limits
Conductor CSA not adequate for circuit length
Excessive voltage drop causing equipment malfunction
regulations.recommended_action: Increase conductor size or reduce circuit length to bring voltage drop within permitted limits.

regulations.section: Section 5 - Selection and Erection of Equipment
regulations.title: Electrical connections
regulations.description: Every connection between conductors and between conductors and equipment shall provide durable electrical continuity and adequate mechanical strength.
common_observations: Poor connections showing signs of overheating
Loose terminations
Connections not properly made or secured
Evidence of scorching at connections
regulations.recommended_action: Inspect, clean and re-tighten all connections. Replace any damaged termination equipment.

regulations.section: Section 5 - Selection and Erection of Equipment
regulations.title: Accessible connections
regulations.description: Every connection shall be accessible for inspection, testing and maintenance except for certain specified joints.
common_observations: Junction boxes buried or inaccessible
Connections not accessible for inspection
Joints concealed without maintenance access
regulations.recommended_action: Provide access to all concealed connections or replace with maintenance-free joints where permitted.

regulations.section: Section 5 - Selection and Erection of Equipment
regulations.title: Selection of RCDs - Type
regulations.description: RCDs shall be selected to ensure correct operation with the type of residual current that may occur.
common_observations: Type AC RCD used where Type A is required
Incorrect RCD type for the connected load
Equipment producing DC fault current without Type B RCD
regulations.recommended_action: Install the correct type of RCD (Type A or Type B) appropriate for the connected equipment.

regulations.section: Section 5 - Selection and Erection of Equipment
regulations.title: RCD - Unwanted tripping
regulations.description: Measures shall be taken to minimise the risk of unwanted tripping of RCDs due to excessive protective conductor currents.
common_observations: Frequent nuisance tripping of RCD
Too many circuits on a single RCD
Standing earth leakage current excessive
regulations.recommended_action: Redistribute circuits across multiple RCDs or install RCBOs to reduce nuisance tripping.

regulations.section: Section 5 - Selection and Erection of Equipment
regulations.title: Isolation - Means of isolation
regulations.description: Every installation shall have a means of isolation from every source of electrical energy.
common_observations: No suitable means of isolation provided
Main switch not suitable for isolation
Unable to isolate entire installation
Isolator not accessible
regulations.recommended_action: Provide suitable means of isolation for every source of supply and ensure accessibility.

regulations.section: Section 5 - Selection and Erection of Equipment
regulations.title: Switching for mechanical maintenance
regulations.description: Suitable means of switching shall be provided for equipment requiring maintenance involving a risk of physical injury.
common_observations: No local isolator for mechanical equipment
Isolator not adjacent to equipment
Unable to lock off supply to equipment
regulations.recommended_action: Provide a local means of isolation adjacent to equipment requiring mechanical maintenance.

regulations.section: Section 5 - Selection and Erection of Equipment
regulations.title: Earthing arrangements
regulations.description: The earthing arrangement shall be verified as suitable for the type of system earthing.
common_observations: Earthing arrangement not suitable or not verified
Earth electrode resistance too high for TT system
Main earthing terminal not provided
regulations.recommended_action: Verify and rectify earthing arrangements to ensure suitability for the system type.

regulations.section: Section 5 - Selection and Erection of Equipment
regulations.title: Protective conductors - Cross-sectional area
regulations.description: The cross-sectional area of protective conductors shall comply with Table 54.7 or be calculated.
common_observations: Protective conductor undersized
CPC cross-sectional area inadequate
Earth conductor does not meet Table 54.7 requirements
regulations.recommended_action: Replace undersized protective conductors with conductors of adequate cross-sectional area per Table 54.7.

regulations.section: Section 5 - Selection and Erection of Equipment
regulations.title: Main earthing terminal
regulations.description: The main earthing terminal shall be provided for the connection of protective conductors, bonding conductors and the earthing conductor.
common_observations: Main earthing terminal not provided
Main earth bar corroded or damaged
Insufficient capacity at main earthing terminal
regulations.recommended_action: Provide a suitable main earthing terminal with adequate capacity for all connections.

regulations.section: Section 5 - Selection and Erection of Equipment
regulations.title: Earthing conductor
regulations.description: The earthing conductor shall have a cross-sectional area not less than specified in Table 54.8.
common_observations: Earthing conductor undersized
Earthing conductor not protected against corrosion
Earthing conductor not adequately connected
regulations.recommended_action: Replace earthing conductor with one of adequate size per Table 54.8 and ensure proper connection and protection.

regulations.section: Section 5 - Selection and Erection of Equipment
regulations.title: Main protective bonding conductors
regulations.description: Main protective bonding conductors shall connect to the main earthing terminal all extraneous-conductive-parts including water, gas and other metallic services.
common_observations: Main bonding not connected to all extraneous-conductive-parts
Main bonding to water service missing
Main bonding to gas service missing
Main bonding conductor undersized
Bonding connection corroded or loose
Main bonding connected incorrectly (wrong side of meter)
regulations.recommended_action: Provide main protective bonding conductors to all extraneous-conductive-parts (water, gas, oil, structural steelwork) with adequate cross-sectional area.

regulations.section: Section 5 - Selection and Erection of Equipment
regulations.title: Main bonding conductor size
regulations.description: The cross-sectional area of main bonding conductors shall be not less than half the required cross-sectional area of the earthing conductor, subject to a minimum of 6mm2 Cu.
common_observations: Main bonding conductors undersized (less than 10mm2 for TN-C-S)
Main bonding less than 6mm2
Bonding conductors do not meet minimum size requirements
regulations.recommended_action: Replace undersized main bonding conductors with 10mm2 copper minimum for TN-C-S supplies or 6mm2 for TN-S.

regulations.title: Supplementary equipotential bonding
regulations.description: Where the disconnection time cannot be achieved, supplementary equipotential bonding shall be provided.
common_observations: Supplementary bonding missing in bathroom
Supplementary bonding not connected to all extraneous-conductive-parts in bathroom
Supplementary bonding undersized
regulations.recommended_action: Provide supplementary equipotential bonding connecting all extraneous-conductive-parts and exposed-conductive-parts within the location.

regulations.title: Continuity of protective conductors
regulations.description: A continuity test shall be carried out on every protective conductor including main and supplementary bonding conductors.
common_observations: Continuity of protective conductor not confirmed
High resistance on earth continuity test
Protective conductor continuity test failed
regulations.recommended_action: Investigate and rectify any failure or high resistance in protective conductor continuity.

regulations.title: Ring final circuit continuity
regulations.description: A ring final circuit test shall verify that each conductor is continuous throughout the ring.
common_observations: Ring final circuit found broken (operating as radial)
Ring continuity readings not balanced
Multiple spurs creating imbalanced ring
regulations.recommended_action: Investigate and repair broken ring final circuits to restore ring continuity.

regulations.title: Insulation resistance
regulations.description: Insulation resistance between live conductors and earth shall be not less than the values stated in Table 61.
common_observations: Insulation resistance below 1 megohm
Low insulation resistance indicating deterioration
Insulation resistance below minimum value
regulations.recommended_action: Investigate low insulation resistance and repair or replace cables with degraded insulation.

regulations.title: Earth fault loop impedance
regulations.description: Earth fault loop impedance shall be measured to verify disconnection time requirements are met.
common_observations: Earth fault loop impedance exceeds maximum permitted value
Zs value too high for protective device
High earth fault loop impedance preventing disconnection within required time
regulations.recommended_action: Reduce earth fault loop impedance or provide additional RCD protection to ensure disconnection within the required time.

regulations.title: RCD test
regulations.description: Where RCDs are fitted, operation shall be verified by testing.
common_observations: RCD failed to operate within required time
RCD tripping time exceeds 300ms at rated residual current
RCD test button not operating correctly
regulations.recommended_action: Replace the faulty RCD with one that operates within the required disconnection time.

regulations.title: Polarity test
regulations.description: A polarity test shall verify that single-pole switching devices are connected in the line conductor only.
common_observations: Single-pole switch connected in neutral conductor
Reversed polarity at socket-outlet
Cross-polarity identified at lighting point
regulations.recommended_action: Correct polarity by reconnecting single-pole devices in the line conductor.

regulations.title: Prospective fault current
regulations.description: The prospective fault current shall be measured at the origin and at every relevant point in the installation.
common_observations: Prospective fault current not measured
Protective devices not rated for prospective fault current
PFC exceeds protective device breaking capacity
regulations.recommended_action: Measure prospective fault current and ensure all protective devices have adequate breaking capacity.

regulations.title: Periodic inspection and testing
regulations.description: Periodic inspection and testing shall be carried out to determine whether the installation is in a satisfactory condition for continued use.
common_observations: Installation has not been periodically inspected within the recommended interval
Previous periodic inspection recommendations not actioned
No evidence of previous EICR
regulations.recommended_action: Carry out periodic inspection and testing at recommended intervals and action any recommendations.

regulations.title: Bathrooms - Additional protection
regulations.description: All circuits in locations containing a bath or shower shall be protected by a 30mA RCD.
common_observations: Bathroom circuit not protected by 30mA RCD
Electric shower without RCD protection
Lighting circuit in bathroom not RCD protected
regulations.recommended_action: Provide 30mA RCD protection for all circuits in the bathroom/shower room.

regulations.title: Bathrooms - Equipment zones
regulations.description: Equipment shall only be installed in zones 0, 1 and 2 if it is suitable for the conditions in that zone.
common_observations: Equipment not suitable for bathroom zone in which it is installed
Socket-outlet installed within Zone 2
Non-IP rated equipment in Zone 1
Equipment in Zone 0 not suitable
regulations.recommended_action: Replace equipment with items having the correct IP rating for the zone or relocate outside the zone.

regulations.title: Bathrooms - Supplementary bonding
regulations.description: Local supplementary bonding may be omitted where all circuits comply with the requirements of Regulation 411.3.3 and are protected by a 30mA RCD.
common_observations: Supplementary bonding missing and circuits not all RCD protected
Not all bathroom circuits on 30mA RCD therefore supplementary bonding required
Supplementary bonding connections loose or corroded
regulations.recommended_action: Either provide supplementary bonding or ensure all bathroom circuits are protected by 30mA RCDs.

regulations.title: Swimming pools - Protection
regulations.description: All circuits supplying equipment in zones 0, 1 and 2 of swimming pools shall be protected by SELV at 12V AC or 30V DC or 30mA RCD.
common_observations: Swimming pool equipment not suitably protected
Incorrect IP rating for pool zone equipment
Lack of RCD protection for pool circuits
regulations.recommended_action: Ensure all swimming pool circuits are protected by SELV or 30mA RCD and equipment has the required IP rating.

regulations.title: Electric vehicle charging installations
regulations.description: Installations for electric vehicle charging shall comply with the requirements of Section 722.
common_observations: EV charging point not on dedicated circuit
EV charger without Type A or Type B RCD protection
No isolation switch for EV charging point
EV circuit not correctly rated
regulations.recommended_action: Ensure EV charging installation complies with Section 722 including dedicated circuit, appropriate RCD type and isolation switch.

regulations.title: Solar photovoltaic supply systems
regulations.description: Installations of solar PV systems shall comply with the requirements of Section 712.
common_observations: PV system warning labels missing
PV system isolator not accessible
PV system not compliant with Section 712
DC side of PV not adequately protected
regulations.recommended_action: Ensure PV installation complies with Section 712, all warning labels are present and isolation is accessible.

regulations.title: Downlighters - Fire risk
regulations.description: Recessed luminaires including downlighters shall be installed with regard to the fire resistance of the building elements and thermal effects.
common_observations: Recessed downlighters in direct contact with thermal insulation
Non-fire-rated downlighters installed in fire-rated ceiling
No fire hoods fitted to recessed luminaires
Insulation packed around downlighters creating fire risk
regulations.recommended_action: Install fire-rated downlighters or fit appropriate fire hoods and ensure adequate separation from thermal insulation.

regulations.section: Section 5 - Selection and Erection of Equipment
regulations.title: Outdoor lighting installations
regulations.description: Outdoor lighting installations shall be protected against damage and suitable for external conditions.
common_observations: Outdoor light fittings not suitable for external use
IP rating not adequate for outdoor installation
External cable entries not sealed
regulations.recommended_action: Replace outdoor lighting with fittings having adequate IP rating for the location.

regulations.title: ADS - Protective equipotential bonding
regulations.description: Main protective bonding conductors shall be provided connecting the main earthing terminal to extraneous-conductive-parts.
common_observations: Main protective bonding missing or inadequate
No bonding to incoming water service
No bonding to incoming gas service
Bonding conductor undersized for supply
regulations.recommended_action: Provide main protective bonding to all extraneous-conductive-parts (water, gas, oil) in accordance with Table 54.8.

regulations.section: Section 5 - Selection and Erection of Equipment
regulations.title: Emergency switching
regulations.description: A means of emergency switching shall be provided where it may be necessary to disconnect the supply to remove an unexpected danger.
common_observations: No emergency switch-off provided where required
Emergency switch not clearly identified or accessible
Main switch difficult to access in emergency
regulations.recommended_action: Provide readily accessible means of emergency switching clearly identified and accessible.

regulations.section: Section 5 - Selection and Erection of Equipment
regulations.title: TN system earthing
regulations.description: For TN systems, means of earthing shall be provided by the connection of the installation earthing conductor to the distributor's earth terminal.
common_observations: Earthing conductor not connected to distributor's earth
Earth terminal not provided by distributor
Earthing arrangement deteriorated
regulations.recommended_action: Verify earthing conductor is correctly connected to the distributor's earth terminal and in good condition.

regulations.section: Section 5 - Selection and Erection of Equipment
regulations.title: Protective conductors - Gas and water pipes
regulations.description: A gas pipe, oil pipe or water pipe shall not be used as a protective conductor.
common_observations: Gas or water pipe being used as an earth conductor
Earth continuity relying on metallic pipework
Protective conductor terminated on gas/water pipe
regulations.recommended_action: Provide a dedicated protective conductor and do not rely on gas or water pipes for earth continuity.
